description: Best practices and conventions for Shopify Dawn-based theme development. See also: https://shopify.dev/docs/themes/best-practices

dawn_updates.rule: We do not track Dawn updates. If a Dawn component updates we will not auto-replace ours. Adopt new Dawn functionality case-by-case only.
dawn_updates.rationale: Avoids backwards-compatibility burden and gives freedom to replace Dawn styles with Tailwind.

core_inventory.rule: Always check the CORE inventory Notion doc before building a new component. If the component is listed, it is a candidate for backport to CORE.
steps: Check CORE inventory doc for a matching component.
If it exists in CORE already, use it instead of building from scratch.
If it does not exist in CORE, build it on the client site first, then create a CORE branch to backport.

build_tools.css: Use Tailwind's build tools for CSS.
build_tools.js: No build tools for JS — plain ES6 files.

feature_gating.rule: When adding a feature, decide if ALL clients will want it.

patterns.case: Feature most clients will not want, but should be user-togglable
patterns.approach: Add a section/block setting to enable or disable the feature.
Add a Liquid variable (e.g. {% assign enable_feature = false %}) at the top of the file.

component_reuse.rule: Check if a Dawn component already does most of what you need. Use it as a base before building from scratch.

tailwind_ui.rule: If Dawn does not suffice or you want Tailwind, use Tailwind UI as a base for markup and styles.

site_wide_settings.rule: Respect all site-wide Liquid variables and settings. Document any your component uses in CORE PR descriptions.

client_specific_code.rule: Avoid hard-coding client-specific references. Flag any that exist when backporting to CORE.

portability.rule: A section should be responsible for itself where possible. Think in components.

rules: Use translation strings for all user-facing text (https://shopify.dev/docs/themes/architecture/locales/storefront-locale-files).
Include collection URL in product URLs where possible.
markets_and_translations.dawn_cull: If a Dawn component has functionality very few clients will want, remove it.

snippet_doc_comment.rule: Every snippet must have a comment at the top covering: (1) name + description, (2) accepted variables with types and (optional) marker, (3) example {% render %} call.

tailwind_abstraction.rule: Tailwind punishes bad abstraction. Never duplicate the same utility class list on multiple elements.
strategies: Extract repeated elements into their own snippet so the class list only exists once.
If a snippet cannot be extracted, create a CSS class and use @apply to apply Tailwind utilities.

prefer_single_file.rule: Keep component styles inside the Liquid file using Tailwind classes. Avoid separate .css files unless absolutely necessary.

no_arbitrary_values.rule: Avoid using Tailwind arbitrary values (e.g. tw-p-[1.2rem]). Use the closest default Tailwind scale value.
no_arbitrary_values.exception: Confirm with the designer first — values that require [ ] are usually a design error.

no_parent_arbitrary_selectors.rule: Do not apply arbitrary [ ] values on parent selectors (e.g. [&>*]:tw-mb-4). Apply classes directly to each child element instead.

js_only_when_necessary.rule: Only use JS where absolutely necessary. Many interactions can be handled with HTML/CSS alone (e.g. <details>/<summary> for toggles).

single_purpose_files.rule: One JS file per Liquid file or per single conceptual task. Keep it small and focused.

jsdoc_typing.rule: Use JSDoc type annotations instead of TypeScript for type safety.

iife_for_global_code.rule: Wrap top-level JS (code not already inside a function or class) in an IIFE.

inline_js_preference.rule: If a file is only used once per page, prefer keeping JS inside the Liquid file.

early_returns.rule: Keep code flat. Use early returns instead of nested if statements.

dry.rule: Abstract repeated logic into functions. Do not repeat yourself.

rules: Prefer higher-order array methods (filter, map, find) over manual loops where readable.
Use includes() for both string and array membership checks. Do not use contains().
Only use let when the variable will be reassigned.

arrow_functions.rule: Prefer arrow functions instead of binding this.

web_components.rule: Use the Web Components API where applicable. Use data attributes (dataset) for state.

dom_selection.rule: Use querySelector / querySelectorAll for DOM selection.

steps: 1. Check CORE inventory to see if the component already exists.
2. Build the component on the client site (see figma-to-liquid workflow).
3. Create a branch on the CORE repo with your component.
4. Remove all client-specific code from the CORE branch.
5. Confirm all data the component requires is present in CORE or your branch.
6. Create a PR into CORE main. PR description must include: theme URL, setting descriptions, variable descriptions.
7. Flag the PR for review on Slack.
8. Complete any change requests from reviewers.
9. Once approved, merge and close the feature branch.
